mission.title: Install and Configure Xregistry on Kubernetes
mission.description: xRegistry is a metadata registry that helps in discovering, validating, and versioning resources in distributed systems. Installing xRegistry allows you to manage and interact with your resources effectively using its server and CLI tools.

steps.title: Clone the xRegistry repository
steps.description:
Start by cloning the xRegistry server repository to your local machine. This will allow you to access the necessary Kubernetes manifests.
```bash
git clone https://github.com/xregistry/server.git
cd server
```

steps.title: Apply the Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Deploy the xRegistry server to your Kubernetes cluster using the provided manifests.
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/ --create-namespace --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the xRegistry pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the xRegistry service
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the xRegistry service to access it via your browser or CLI.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/xregistry-service --namespace xregistry 8080:80
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the xRegistry deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/xregistry --namespace xregistry --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi --requests=cpu=250m,memory=256Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the xRegistry deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the xRegistry deployment and its associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete -f k8s/ --namespace xregistry
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the xRegistry namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the xRegistry namespace to clean up any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace xregistry
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description: Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data if necessary. This may involve exporting resources or taking snapshots.

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the xRegistry repository to get the updated manifests.
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated Kubernetes manifests to upgrade the xRegistry server.
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/ --namespace xregistry
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure that the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the xRegistry pods are stuck in the Pending state, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the xRegistry service, ensure that the service is running and the port-forwarding is set up correctly.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace xregistry
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff errors
troubleshooting.description:
If you see CrashLoopBackOff errors in your pods, check the logs for errors that may indicate misconfiguration.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace xregistry
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of xRegistry will result in running pods in the xregistry namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the port-forwarded local port. Ensure that you can interact with the CLI as well.

prerequisites.description: You need a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed on your machine.
